notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 2, Book 9 source file.
Developing card: moderate length with more vocabulary and plot detail.

book.title: The Tale of Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle
book.description: The Tale of Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: moderate length with more vocabulary and plot detail

weeks.title: The Tale of Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle Book Card
vocabulary: 1. troublesome - causing problems or difficulties; bothersome
2. distressed - upset and worried; feeling troubled
2. overwhelming - very strong or powerful; hard to resist
1. remarkable - amazing or surprising; worthy of notice
2. encountered - met or came across unexpectedly
2. triumphantly - in a proud and victorious way
2. hospitality - friendly and generous treatment of guests
1. bristly - rough and prickly; having stiff hairs
2. transformed - changed completely from one thing to another
journal: Write a thank-you note to Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle from one of the animals.
Describe Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle using three character traits and evidence.
writing: Write a thank-you note to Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle from one of the animals.
Describe Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle using three character traits and evidence.
questions: 2. What else had Lucie lost besides her handkerchiefs?
1. What did Lucie notice in the mud?
2. What sounds did Lucie hear as she climbed higher?
1. What did Lucie find at the top of the hill?
1. Whose clothes did Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle show to Lucie first?
2. How did Lucie's pinafore look after Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle washed it?
1. What did Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle serve with tea?
2. What did Lucie learn about the woodland animals?
1. What happened to Mrs. Tiggy-Winkle as they walked down the hill?
2. How did Lucie know her adventure was real?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
